firstStep: Open the service page or ask your GP to connect you with the right community team.

name: Pact West Coast
hours: Ask about current West Coast mental health and addiction support options.
cost: Ask about funded community mental health and addiction support.
fit: Community mental health and addiction support provider referenced in West Coast pathways, including parent and caregiver mental health support.
firstStep: Call the West Coast number or open the Pact website and ask for the local support team.

name: Homebuilders West Coast
hours: Ask about current family, youth, counselling, and practical support options.
cost: Counselling includes up to ten free sessions through listed programmes.
fit: West Coast family and counselling support for children, adults, whanau, youth and people wanting to move away from methamphetamine or other drugs.
firstStep: Call, email, or use the referral form and ask about counselling or family support.

name: Rata Alcohol and Other Drugs Service
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:30am-4pm.
cost: Free public alcohol and other drug support.
fit: Health NZ alcohol and drug service with assessment, counselling, referral to detox or residential support, and links with local NGOs across the West Coast.
firstStep: Call 0800 006 225 or walk in to ask about AOD support.

name: West Coast Youth Online Programme
hours: Online youth support; ask about referral and current availability.
cost: Free online counselling and psychotherapy for eligible youth.
fit: Free online support, counselling and psychotherapy for West Coast young people aged 12 to 24 with mild to moderate mental health issues.
firstStep: Open the programme page and ask how to access Youth Online support.

name: Te Kaika Toku Oranga
hours: GP-linked appointments; often same day where available.
cost: Free Access and Choice mental health and addiction support.
fit: Free Access and Choice support through Te Kaika for stress, anxiety, sleep, long-term health issues and addiction recovery in Dunedin and Oamaru general practices.
firstStep: Ask your practice whether Te Kaika Toku Oranga support is available, or contact Te Kaika for guidance.

name: WellSouth Toku Oranga Access and Choice
hours: Access through participating general practices.
cost: Free 20-30 minute wellbeing appointments where available.
fit: Southern Access and Choice programme with health improvement practitioners, health coaches and community support workers in many Otago general practices.

name: Mirror Services
hours: Ask about current youth counselling, AOD, and referral options.
cost: Ask about free or funded youth mental health and addiction support.
fit: Youth-focused counselling and alcohol and other drug support in Otago, useful when someone wants a rangatahi-centred first step.

name: Adventure Development Otago
hours: Ask about current Otago youth support and referral options.
cost: Ask about free or funded youth wellbeing, mental health, or AOD support.

name: OCASA
hours: Ask about current counselling, crisis, and referral options.
cost: Ask about free or funded sexual harm support and ACC Sensitive Claims pathways.
fit: Otago support for people affected by sexual harm, useful when trauma or sexual harm is part of what is happening.

cost: Youth one-stop-shop wellbeing services; ask about free or low-cost access.
